rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Records Responsive To Business Records Subpoena And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Special Administrator William C. Lynch's Motion to Compel Production of Records Responsive to Business Records Subpoena and Request for Monetary Sanctions. Lynch brings this motion under Code of Civil Procedure sections 1987.1 and 2025.480, and seeks monetary sanctions under Code of Civil procedure section 2023.030, in the amount of $2,000. Lynch was appointed Special Administrator of the Estate of Mako U. Rova on February 9, 2022, with bond in the amount of $5,000. Letters of Special Administration issued the next day, February 10, 2022, with expiration date of March 2, 2022. Lynch served a business records subpoena on Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach on June 1, 2022, with a June 17, 2022 deadline seeking production of "Any and all DOCUMENTS and/or files, whether complete or partial, referencing [Decedent's] estate plan, a revocable living trust, a trust, a will, or a testamentary instrument." (Attachment 3 to Subpoena.) On June 20, 2022, Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach returned the form SUBP-025 Objection by Non-Party to Production of Records, objecting to the entire subpoena on the following grounds: "Work product - CCP 2018.030; Attorney-Client Privilege - Evidence Code [sections] 952, 953; State Bar Rule 1.6 & Business and Professi [sic]." No opposition to this motion was filed with the court. No reply or notice of non-opposition was filed with the court. There is no indication before the court that any of the subpoenaed records have been produced. Exhibits attached to Declaration of Joanna Hohenstein in Support of this motion indicate that Lynch and Dean Ito Taylor, Director of Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach, both claim to be the current trustee of Decedent's trust. As a preliminary matter, under Probate Code section 8200(a), "[T]he custodian of a will shall, within 30 days after having knowledge of the death of the testator, do both of the following: (1) Deliver the will, personally or by registered or certified mail, to the clerk of the superior court of the county in which the estate of the decedent may be administered. (2) Deliver a copy of the will pursuant to Section 1215 to the person named in the will as executor, if the person's whereabouts is known to the custodian, or if not, to a person named in the will as a beneficiary, if the person's whereabouts is known to the custodian." Under Probate Code section 8200(b), "A custodian of a will who fails to comply with the requirements of this section shall be liable for all damages sustained by any person injured by the failure." Special Administrator William C. Lynch's unopposed Motion to Compel Production of Records Responsive to Business Records Subpoena is GRANTED. Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach is ordered to produce all documents in its possession and control that are responsive to the Business Records Subpoena Lynch served on June 1, 2022, within 15 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Lynch's Request for Monetary Sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.480 is DENIED. There was no opposition to this motion to compel production (see, Code of Civil Proc. section 2025.480(j)). [Part 1 of 2]

[Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Verified Petition For Order Confirming Personal Property To Petitioner, Decedent'S Wife
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a file stamped courtesy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. File stamped courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. A hard copy of the proposed order must be provided to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
